text: We measure H0 = 73.4 +/- 1.1 km/s/Mpc from the Cepheid-calibrated SNe Ia sample.
Planck 2018 reports H_0 = 67.4 \pm 0.5 km/s/Mpc consistent with Lambda-CDM.
Our combined likelihood gives H_{0} = 70.0 \pm 1.2 km/s/Mpc.
The TRGB calibration yields H0 = 69.8 \pm 0.6 km/s/Mpc with reduced systematics.
From strong lensing time delays we obtain H_0 = 73.3 ^{+1.7}_{-1.8} km/s/Mpc.
Megamaser distances give H0 = 73.9 \pm 3.0 km/s/Mpc.
The SH0ES collaboration finds H_0 = 73.04 \pm 1.04 km/s/Mpc using Cepheid+SNe.
Our gravitational-wave standard-siren analysis gives H0 = 70.0 ^{+12.0}_{-8.0} km/s/Mpc.
Combining BAO + BBN, we infer H_0 = 67.5 \pm 1.1 km/s/Mpc.
The local distance ladder calibration gives H0 = 74.03 \pm 1.42 km/s/Mpc.
We derive H_0 = 67.36 \pm 0.54 km/s/Mpc from the full Planck dataset.
The model-independent inverse-distance ladder yields H0 = 68.1 \pm 1.5 km/s/Mpc.
Late-time tracker quintessence shifts H_0 = 71.5 \pm 1.3 km/s/Mpc upward.
Our reanalysis prefers H0 = 72.5 ± 1.6 km/s/Mpc using Cepheid recalibration.
BAO-only reconstruction gives H_0 = 67.9 ± 1.4 km/s/Mpc independent of CMB.
Surface-brightness fluctuation distances yield H0 = 73.3 ± 0.7 km/s/Mpc.
We adopt H0 = 70 km/s/Mpc throughout the paper for consistency with prior work.
Joint TT+TE+EE analysis returns H_0 = 66.88 ± 0.92 km/s/Mpc.
Time-delay cosmography from quasar lenses gives H0 = 71.5 ^{+2.4}_{-2.2} km/s/Mpc.
Angular BAO analysis returns H_0 = 69.1 \pm 1.2 km/s/Mpc.
We obtain Omega_m = 0.315 ± 0.007 from the joint Planck+BAO fit.
Our SNe Ia compilation yields Omega_m = 0.298 \pm 0.022 in flat LCDM.
Weak lensing gives \Omega_m = 0.305 \pm 0.012 consistent with CMB.
DES Year-3 finds \Omega_{m} = 0.339 ^{+0.032}_{-0.031} from clustering+lensing.
BOSS galaxies give Omega_m = 0.310 \pm 0.010 in good agreement with Planck.
We assume Omega_m = 0.30 throughout this work.
Combining BBN+BAO yields Omega_m = 0.299 ± 0.011 with weak prior dependence.
Cluster abundance analysis gives \Omega_m = 0.314 ^{+0.018}_{-0.020}.
Pantheon+ supernovae prefer Omega_M = 0.334 \pm 0.018 in flat-LCDM.
WMAP nine-year analysis returned Omega_m = 0.279 \pm 0.025.
Cosmic chronometers favor Omega_m = 0.32 ± 0.04 with no model assumption.
Lensing tomography finds \Omega_m = 0.290 \pm 0.030 consistent with literature.
Ω_m = 0.315 ± 0.008 from the combined dataset.
Joint analysis of SNe + BAO produces Omega_m = 0.302 ± 0.015 in 1-sigma.
We constrain Omega_m = 0.327 \pm 0.018 from full-shape galaxy clustering.
Our analysis gives sigma_8 = 0.811 \pm 0.006 in agreement with Planck.
KiDS-1000 finds sigma_8 = 0.737 ^{+0.040}_{-0.036} in cosmic shear.
DES Y3 cosmic shear gives \sigma_8 = 0.733 \pm 0.039 with shear systematics marginalised.
Cluster counts return sigma8 = 0.78 ± 0.02 in tension with CMB.
Lyman-alpha forest yields \sigma_{8} = 0.825 \pm 0.021 at z~3.
Combining lensing and clustering gives sigma_8 = 0.762 \pm 0.024.
Planck-derived sigma_8 = 0.8111 \pm 0.0060 from the base model fit.
Reanalysed cluster catalog gives sigma_8 = 0.793 ± 0.025 with HSE bias allowed.
We obtain σ_8 = 0.776 ± 0.017 in our fiducial setup.
Our redshift-space distortion measurement gives sigma_8 = 0.79 \pm 0.04.
Planck reports Omega_b = 0.0493 \pm 0.0006 from the base-LCDM fit.
We measure \Omega_b = 0.0486 \pm 0.0010 from joint CMB+BBN.
BBN-only constraint yields Omega_b = 0.0224 ± 0.0005 (physical density).
Joint analysis gives Omega_Lambda = 0.685 \pm 0.007 in flat LCDM.
We find \Omega_\Lambda = 0.692 ± 0.012 from supernova compilation alone.
